requirements:
1- Comprehensive Medical History and physical examination findings, highlighting the indication for the MRI scan and relevant past medical and surgical history.
2- Details of the trauma history relevant to the current complaint, if found.
3- Clear provisional diagnosis.
4- Related lab results support the suspected diagnosis, if found.
5- Initial or previous relevant imaging studies (e.g. US / X-ray), if found.

1- Comprehensive Medical History and physical examination findings, highlighting the indication for the CT scan and relevant past medical and surgical history.
2- Details of the trauma history relevant to the current complaint, if found.
3- Clear provisional diagnosis.
4- Related lab results support the suspected diagnosis, if found.

1- HbA1c: History of relevant Present illness or medication history &/or RBS or FBS Reports if available.
2- Vit D: The medical indication for vitamin D testing. Please mention or submit the relevant evidence, if applicable.
3- FSH and LH: Initial Prolactin and TSH lab reports, clinical examination (specify if relevant to irregular menstruation, infertility, PCOS, Premature Ovarian Failure, menopause or others).
4- T4, T3: TSH lab report (in suspected cases of primary hypothyroidism).
5- Ferritin and iron: CBC lab report, detailed history of PHI, Medication history, relevant physical examination findings (not applied in cases of iron overload).

1- A detailed medical history, highlighting the duration of illness, the primary cause of patient's complaint and the initial management provided.
2- Physical examination findings (e.g. range of motion, reflexes, strength, ...etc).
3- Radiology report (e.g. Xray or CT or MRI).
4- A detailed proposed treatment plan (the physical therapy modalities will be followed, the number of required sessions and the Goals of treatment) along with follow-up assessment report.

A- Documented report demonstrating treatment attempts with multiple daily injections for at least 6 months, including drug prescriptions.
B- Documented blood glucose readings for the last two months.
C- Documented medical visit reports highlighting attempts to adjust insulin administration and self-management during the visits.
D- A documented report confirming that the patient has completed a diabetes education training program satisfactorily, including self-care processes and follow-up procedures.
E- A minimum of two HbA1c lab results, each taken recently (within the last 3 months) and spaced at least 3 months apart.

1- Relevant laboratory and imaging tests support the indication (e.g. FOBT, calprotectin, CT, barium enema ...etc).
